LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER CITY OF MILPITAS' PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED. THE MOTION IS DENIED IN ALL RESPECTS. UPON REVIEW OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, COURT FINDS THAT CALRECYCLE LACKS JURISDICTION REGARDING ORDER INSURANCES. ISSUES RAISED IN THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION HAVE BEEN COVERED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER CITY OF MILPITAS' HEARING ON WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED BY THE COURT TO MAY 31, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302. THE PARTIES TO DRAFT A STIPULATION AND ORDER THAT WOULD BE SERVED ON THE BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT ON HOW THEY WOULD PARTICIPATE. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE, THEY CAN CONTACT THE COURT BY WAY OF E-MAIL OR LETTER. THE COURT PREFERRABLY WANTS TO SEE A WRITTEN POSITION OF THE AIR DISTRICT AND THE POSITION OF ALL THE PARTIES IN THE NEXT HEARING. A RESPONSE MAY BE FILED, IF CALRECYCLE WILL MAINTAIN ITS VIEW THAT THE CERIFICATION IS CONSTRAINED BY EXISTING REGULATION AND THEREFORE THERE IS NO ROOM FOR CERTIFICATION PROCESS AS FRAMED IN THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION. A BRIEFING SCHEDULE MAY BE SET BY THE PARTIES. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: LAURA A. REDING, CSR 	711 =302/HK

Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kelly T. Smith of the law offices of The Smith Law Firm (916-930-1961) appears as counsel for petitioner, City of Milpitas

Deputy Attorney General, Janelle M. Smith of the California Department of Justice (415-703-5521) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party, CalRecycle

Attorney, Thomas M. Bruen of the law offices of Thomas M. Bruen (925-295-3137) appears as counsel for real party-in-interest, International Disposal Corporation of California

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

PETITIONER CITY OF MILPITAS' Petition for Writ Of Mandate is DENIED in all respects. Upon review of the administrative record, Court finds that CalRecycle lacks jurisdiction regarding orderinsuurances. Issues raised in the second cause of action have been covered. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Apr-12-2017 Continued To May-31-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KELLY T. SMITH OF THE SMITH FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-916-930-1961 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. JANELLE M. SMITH, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 1-415-703-5521;
ATTY. THOMAS M. BRUEN, TEL. NO. 1-925-295-3037 APPEARING FO THE REAL PARTIES IN INTEREST INTERNATIONAL DISPOSAL CORPORATION ET. AL.

RULING - A Stipulation and Order to Supplement Adminisrative Record was signed by the court before arguments were heard. The matter was argued. PETITIONER CITY OF MILPITAS' Hearing on Writ Of Mandate is continued by the court to May 31, 2017. The parties to draft a stipulation and order that would be served on the Bay Area Air Quality Management District on how they would participate. If the parties cannot agree, they can contact the court by way of e-mail or letter. The court preferrably wants to see a written position of the air district and the position of all the parties in the next hearing. A Response may be filed, if CALRECYCLE will maintain its view that the cerification is constrained by existing regulation and therefore there is no room for certification process as framed in the 2nd cause of action. A Briefing schedule may be set by the parties. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR 711 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Sep-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kelly T. Smith of the The Smith Law Firm (916) 930-1961, appears as counsel for petitioner/opposing party, City of Milpitas

Deputy Attorneys General Janelle M. Smith and Tiffany Yee of the California Department of Justice (415) 703-5521, appear as counsels for respondent/demurring party, CalRecycle

Senior Deputy City Attorney, Margo Laskowska of the San Jose City Attorney's Office (408) 535-1965 appears as counsel for respondent, City of San Jose

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

RESPONDENT CALRECYCLE'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION is OVERRULED as to the first and second causes of action for a writ of mandate and SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for declaratory relief. Even assuming that the BAAQMD has jurisdiction over odor complaints at landfill sites, CalRecycle fails to show that it is not a proper party to this action and/or that it lacks "jurisdiction" to provide the relief sought by petitioner City of Milpitas. Petitioner is complaining about a permit that CalRecycle approved and petitioner correctly notes that CalRecycle is the lead entity responsible for operating and managing the landfill. (27 CCR 21600(b)(7)(A); 27 CCR 20760; Public Resources Code 43020.) Petitioner sufficiently exhausted its administrative remedies and it is arguable that such exhaustion is not even necessary. (No Wetlands Landfill Expansion v. County of Marin (2012) 204 Cal.App.4th 573, 582). The court cannot determine based on this demurrer that the second cause of action is moot. Petitioner's declaratory relief claim fails because its remedy is to pursue its writ claims and it failed to oppose the demurrer as to this claim. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, email: lmartinez@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Sep-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kelly T. Smith of the The Smith Law Firm (916) 930-1961, appears as counsel for petitioner/opposing party, City of Milpitas

Deputy Attorneys General Janelle M. Smith and Tiffany Yee of the California Department of Justice (415) 703-5521, appear as counsels for respondent, CalRecycle

Senior Deputy City Attorney, Margo Laskowska of the San Jose City Attorney's Office (408) 535-1965 appears as counsel for respondent/demurring party, City of San Jose

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

RESPONDENTS CITY OF SAN JOSE DEPARTMENT OF PLANNING AND BUILDING AND CODE ENFORCEMENT'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION is sustained without leave to amend. Petitioner's action seeks review of CalRecycle's final decision and there is no basis to join the City of San Jose, who was the initial decision maker. (Du Four v. Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board (1975) 49 Cal.App.3d 863, 867-868.) Until a public agency makes a "final" decision, the matter is not ripe for judicial review. (San Bernardino Public Employees Association v. City of Fontana (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 1215 1226-1227.) Written order and judgment of dismissal are signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, email: lmartinez@sftc.org. (D302)
